summary: Hannah and Adam from HB Capital present multifamily lending options, underwriting criteria, and borrower requirements for investors. While focused on capital solutions rather than deal analysis, the content addresses financing strategies critical for Brian's 15-50 unit deals in GA/TN/AL. Discussion covers loan products (agency, CMBS, banks, bridge), rate/LTV ranges, experience/liquidity thresholds, and soft quote processes relevant to deal execution.
takeaways: Write aggressive LOIs even if significantly below asking; follow with professional text to broker: 'Hey [broker], I just sent you an LOI on [property]. It's aggressive, but I'm going off the numbers I have. I'd love to chat more about it' to build rapport and access pre-market deals
80% of deals are in pre-market phase before websites; use online listings (Krexy, Loopnet) as lure to build broker relationships, not primary sourcing tools
Target 65-70% LTV in underwriting despite lenders approving higher, because DSCR requirements (typically 1.2-1.35x) naturally back into that range given current market conditions
Local/regional banks and credit unions are sweet spot for 10-50 unit deals; offer 5.5-7.25% rates, 5-10 year terms, more flexibility than CMBS ($2M minimum, rigid, expensive attorneys)
Bridge loans for distressed assets: 9-12% rate, 12-18 month term, 2 points (1% in/1% out), minimum 6-month interest requirement; use when you can buy 30% below value
Workforce housing (B/C class) outperforming Class A in 2026; national rents up 25% since 2020 but affordability at multi-decade lows driving demand to secondary markets

New sponsors: start with duplexes/triplexes or partner with experienced operator taking minority equity; alternatively structure as co-GP with experienced partner who gets larger split to earn experience

skill_candidates.change: Reframe failed LOI attempts as relationship-building, not rejection; send follow-up text after 'aggressive' offers to explain underwriting logic and request conversation
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explicitly coaches on text script: 'Hey John, I just sent you an LOI...It's aggressive, but I'm going off the numbers that I have. I'd love to chat with you more about it' to amplify broker willingness and build rapport for off-market deal flow

skill_candidates.change: Underwrite to 65-70% LTV ceiling rather than maximum lender approval, because DSCR 1.2-1.35x requirements naturally constrain leverage in current market
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin states: 'I have you guys underwrite to 65 70% because I understand they'll go up higher but when you look at the debt coverage ratios and everything based on the property's financials right now as we all see you're going to back into that 65 70% nine times out of 10 right now'

skill_candidates.change: Request soft quote letters from lenders pre-LOI (contingent on personal financial statement + OM) to include with offers and accelerate broker confidence
skill_candidates.evidence: Hannah explains soft quote process: review PFS and OM, run deal calculator, provide rate/LTV estimate 'so you can get your LOI together and have a general idea'; Justin confirms this 'moves the needle quite a bit' with brokers

skill_candidates.change: Prioritize Midwest and coastal markets (Chicago, NY, Kansas City, Twin Cities) showing rent growth; avoid Sunbelt oversupply (Phoenix, Austin, Denver, Tampa) until absorption completes late 2026
skill_candidates.evidence: Hannah presents absorption data: Austin 7.5%, Charlotte 7.4%, Raleigh 6%, Nashville 5.6%, Phoenix 5.3% vs Midwest showing growth; notes 'Sunbelt markets are absorbing their over supply and expected to rebalance late in this year'

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - Real Estate Tip for New Investors - 03/03/26 | Justin Brennan
